All materials need to include true lender language and the WebBank disclosure.

kept.name: Automated Payment Set-up (Incl. ACH Authorization and Capture process)
kept.bank_guidance:
Another consideration is that Consumer accounts and Business accounts have differing ACH Authorization requirements. 
Regulation E is applicable to consumer accounts and is a consumer protection reg. (Business accounts are not regulated under Reg E.) Single ACH Authorization is treated differently that recurring automated EFTs (like ACHs).

kept.name: E-Sign Authorization & Capture Process (Incl. Disclosure, Consent, and email verification)
kept.bank_guidance: Need to ensure ESIGN is consented to prior to delivery of a notice required to be provided in writing (Adverse Action, TILA). Also must ensure reasonable demonstration from the customer that they have the ability to view and retain the notice in the form provided (email, online document portal). Otherwise required notices must be provided in a paper format.

kept.name: Terms of Use
kept.bank_guidance: Includes Terms of Use for website or app

kept.name: Military Service Eligibility Communications (MLA) - MLA disclosures in agreement to also include verbal disclosures and phone number
Credit Policy - Includes: Credit Policy , Credit Policy Change Management Procedure, Ability to Pay calculations, Non-Predatory Lending Assessment (for APR's/Effective Interest Rate > 36%)
kept.bank_guidance: Should provide great detail pertaining to all aspects to credit decisions including pricing, limits, ability-to-pay, and other considerations.

kept.name: Model Governance Policy & Model Inventory List - Includes: Model Governance Policy, Model Development Document, Generic/Off the shelf Model Score Whitepapers (should be included in UW2.2), Fraud Model Documentation (should be included in UW2.2), Any other custom score model documentation (should be included in UW2.2), Model Performance Report, Independent Model Validation Report
kept.bank_guidance: Compliance is becoming more and more involved in the review of models. Review as applicable and work with the Treating Customers Fairly officer and the assigned post-launch Compliance Manager to ensure the appropriate level of compliance review.

kept.name: Income Verification Procedures
kept.bank_guidance:
Does the party rely on stated income? Does the Partner seek access to bank account information, ask for paystubs, or use a 3rd party (such as a credit reporting agency) to confirm applicant's income?
If the product is regulated under TILA and CARD Act - how is Ability to Pay confirmed?
kept.notes: Flex application flow introduces a self-reported Ability-to-Pay assessment

kept.name: Validate APR Calculation (Incl. MAPR if applicable to Product)
Notice of Incompleteness
kept.bank_guidance: Relates directly to whether or not a Notice of Adverse Action must be sent to an applicant that has been declined for an incomplete application. If an NOI, that is in compliance with Reg B's requirements for an NOI, is sent to the applicant and the reason for adverse action is incomplete application, then a Notice of Adverse Action may not be required.

kept.name: Decisions & Reasons - Includes: Decision Communication , AA Methodology , AA Reasons, Adverse Action Communications - Bureau, Adverse Action Communications - Non Bureau, Adverse Action Communications - Manual Delivery Process, ECOA/Adverse Action Reporting, Approval Communication
kept.bank_guidance:
The methodology behind what adverse action descriptions are provided to the applicant. Normally no more than four reasons are shared with the applicant. Only the top and most meaningful reasons are shared.
The verbiage for the AA reasons must be specific and accurate. Many partners receive many  decline reason codes, after the call to the credit reporting agency, these codes may be mapped down to the same adverse action reason. 
Should clearly indicate the Bank is the creditor, with the Partner as the servicer.  Questions regarding a credit bureau pull must be addressed to the appropriate Credit Reporting Agency.
There are model forms available under Reg B and Reg V.
kept.notes:
Post Launch Validation Testing
No new adverse action reasons are being introduced

kept.bank_guidance: A notice that is sent to the applicant to let them know why the applicant did not get approved for the lowest interest rate or other such prime terms. This notice is not required if the FCRA notice related to credit scores is provided.

Two forms of TIL disclosures. There is a general application TIL disclosure and an approved applicant TIL that is specific to the applicant. The approved applicant TIL must be presented to the consumer prior to account opening.
